content:
First, label the board cells by coordinates \((i,j)\) for \(1\le i,j\le3\).  Note that the adjacency graph of the \(3\times3\) grid is bipartite, with the two parts given by the parity of \(i+j\).  A quick count shows the “even‐sum’’ cells \((i+j\equiv0\pmod2)\) are 5 in number, the “odd‐sum’’ cells are 4.  Since we have exactly 5 odd numbers \(\{1,3,5,7,9\}\) and 4 even numbers \(\{2,4,6,8\}\), the only way to ensure every edge joins an odd to an even is to place all 5 odds on the even-sum cells and all 4 evens on the odd-sum cells.

Thus the total number of *Isthmian* fillings is
\[
5!\;\times\;4!\;=\;120\cdot24\;=\;2880.
\]

We now quotient by the action of the cyclic group of rotations \(C_4\).  By Burnside’s Lemma the number of orbits is
\[
\frac1{4}\Bigl(\,\underbrace{2880}_{\text{identity}}+\underbrace{0}_{90^\circ}
+\underbrace{0}_{180^\circ}+\underbrace{0}_{270^\circ}\Bigr)
\;=\;\frac{2880}{4}
\;=\;720.
\]
No nontrivial rotation can fix an arrangement because it would force distinct labels to coincide on different cells.  Hence the final answer is

\boxed{720}

Thus the total number of *Isthmian* fillings is
\[

\]

\[
\frac1{4}\Bigl(\,\underbrace{2880}_{\text{identity}}+\underbrace{0}_{90^\circ}

\boxed{720}
